title: celtis occidentalis var. cordata
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. cordata, also known as American Hackberry Dwarf, is a variety of the deciduous Celtis occidentalis tree, native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include the potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and constipation. Celtis occidentalis var. cordata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
